Okay. Let me try to dig a little deeper.

But it's something I can work on.
Not a proof of fraud.

I can do that.
I keep replaying that last argument.
My words were so sharp.
It’s been six months of silence from my daughter.
I just want to forgive myself.
But the quiet feels like a punishment.
I said she was selfish.
I told her she’d never understand.
It was about her moving across the country.
I was scared of being left alone.

I see that now.
The guilt is heavy every morning.

And yet, here I am.

Maybe I was trying to control her.
Because I felt so out of control.

Instead, I made it about my loneliness.
I think that's the core of it.
My fear of abandonment.
It's an old, deep wound.

It wasn't hers to carry.
I made her carry it anyway.

Without trying to fix it right away.
It feels like a storm inside me.

I keep wanting to earn my way out.

My mother believed in suffering.
